feed.title: Hung Pham
feed.subtitle: A minimal, responsive, and powerful Jekyll theme for presenting documents and notes written by Hung Pham.

entry.title: Upgrade Dell Server Firmware with Ansible

link.title: Upgrade Dell Server Firmware with Ansible

entry.summary: IDRAC VS REDFISH Redfish and iDRAC are both technologies used for remote server management, but they have some differences in terms of features and implementation. Redfish is an open, standard API (Application Programming Interface) for server management that is supported by many hardware vendors. It provides a unified, modern interface for managing servers, storage, and networking equipment,...

entry.title: Azure - Recover VM with Marketplace Plan

link.title: Azure - Recover VM with Marketplace Plan

entry.summary: Recover a VM on Azure If the VM is deleted accidentally Prerequisites: The snapshots for VM disks must be existing. Open Snapshot page on Azure portal UI Create a new disk from snapshot After new disk is created, open portal UI page of the disk Use Create VM option to create new VM Fill in the configuration parameters needed to create the VM Create the VM Associate the old publ...

entry.title: How to setup self-hosted openvpn server

link.title: How to setup self-hosted openvpn server

entry.summary: Prerequisite A VM (ubuntu 20.04) should be available for the following steps. Step 1 - Installing OpenVPN and Easy-RSA sudo apt update sudo apt install openvpn easy-rsa mkdir ~/easy-rsa # create a new directory on the OpenVPN Server as your non-root user ln -s /usr/share/easy-rsa/* ~/easy-rsa/ # create a symlink from the easyrsa script that the package installed into the ~/easy-rsa directory...

entry.title: Network Security Exercises
entry.summary: How to Create a LAB VNET with Docker The system will look like the following diagram: Note: the ip addresses of the VMs are different from the above diagram (also even if you don’t use docker but normal VMs tool like VirtualBox…), but the method stays the same. $ docker network create --driver=bridge --subnet=172.16.2.0/24 --ip-range=172.16.2.0/24 br0 $ docker network create --driver=br...
